clients.name: KeyVaultClient
clients.doc: The key vault client performs cryptographic key operations and vault operations against the Key Vault service.

methods.name: set_secret
methods.doc: The SET operation adds a secret to the Azure Key Vault. If the named secret already exists, Azure Key Vault creates a new version of that secret. This operation requires the secrets/set permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the secret. The value you provide may be copied globally for the purpose of running the service. The value provided should not include personally identifiable or sensitive information.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.doc: The parameters for setting the secret.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.doc: Body parameter's content type. Known values are application/json

methods.name: delete_secret
methods.doc: The DELETE operation applies to any secret stored in Azure Key Vault. DELETE cannot be applied to an individual version of a secret. This operation requires the secrets/delete permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the secret.

methods.name: update_secret
methods.doc: The UPDATE operation changes specified attributes of an existing stored secret. Attributes that are not specified in the request are left unchanged. The value of a secret itself cannot be changed. This operation requires the secrets/set permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the secret.

parameters.name: secret_version
parameters.doc: The version of the secret.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.doc: The parameters for update secret operation.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.doc: Body parameter's content type. Known values are application/json

methods.name: get_secret
methods.doc: The GET operation is applicable to any secret stored in Azure Key Vault. This operation requires the secrets/get permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the secret.

parameters.name: secret_version
parameters.doc: The version of the secret. This URI fragment is optional. If not specified, the latest version of the secret is returned.

parameters.name: out_content_type
parameters.doc: The media type (MIME type) of the certificate. If a supported format is specified, the certificate content is converted to the requested format. Currently, only PFX to PEM conversion is supported. If an unsupported format is specified, the request is rejected. If not specified, the certificate is returned in its original format without conversion.

methods.name: get_secrets
methods.doc: The Get Secrets operation is applicable to the entire vault. However, only the base secret identifier and its attributes are provided in the response. Individual secret versions are not listed in the response. This operation requires the secrets/list permission.

parameters.name: maxresults
parameters.doc: Maximum number of results to return in a page. If not specified the service will return up to 25 results.

methods.name: get_secret_versions
methods.doc: The full secret identifier and attributes are provided in the response. No values are returned for the secrets. This operations requires the secrets/list permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the secret.

parameters.name: maxresults
parameters.doc: Maximum number of results to return in a page. If not specified the service will return up to 25 results.

methods.name: get_deleted_secrets
methods.doc: The Get Deleted Secrets operation returns the secrets that have been deleted for a vault enabled for soft-delete. This operation requires the secrets/list permission.

parameters.name: maxresults
parameters.doc: Maximum number of results to return in a page. If not specified the service will return up to 25 results.

methods.name: get_deleted_secret
methods.doc: The Get Deleted Secret operation returns the specified deleted secret along with its attributes. This operation requires the secrets/get permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the secret.

methods.name: purge_deleted_secret
methods.doc: The purge deleted secret operation removes the secret permanently, without the possibility of recovery. This operation can only be enabled on a soft-delete enabled vault. This operation requires the secrets/purge permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the secret.

methods.name: recover_deleted_secret
methods.doc: Recovers the deleted secret in the specified vault. This operation can only be performed on a soft-delete enabled vault. This operation requires the secrets/recover permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the deleted secret.

methods.name: backup_secret
methods.doc: Requests that a backup of the specified secret be downloaded to the client. All versions of the secret will be downloaded. This operation requires the secrets/backup permission.

parameters.name: secret_name
parameters.doc: The name of the secret.

methods.name: restore_secret
methods.doc: Restores a backed up secret, and all its versions, to a vault. This operation requires the secrets/restore permission.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.doc: The parameters to restore the secret.

parameters.name: content_type
parameters.doc: Body parameter's content type. Known values are application/json

fields.name: value
fields.doc: The value of the secret.

fields.name: tags
fields.doc: Application specific metadata in the form of key-value pairs.

fields.name: content_type
fields.doc: Type of the secret value such as a password.

fields.name: secret_attributes
fields.doc: The secret management attributes.

fields.name: enabled
fields.doc: Determines whether the object is enabled.

fields.name: not_before
fields.doc: Not before date in UTC.

fields.name: expires
fields.doc: Expiry date in UTC.

fields.name: created
fields.doc: Creation time in UTC.

fields.name: updated
fields.doc: Last updated time in UTC.

fields.name: recoverable_days
fields.doc: softDelete data retention days. Value should be >=7 and <=90 when softDelete enabled, otherwise 0.

fields.name: recovery_level
fields.doc: Reflects the deletion recovery level currently in effect for secrets in the current vault. If it contains 'Purgeable', the secret can be permanently deleted by a privileged user; otherwise, only the system can purge the secret, at the end of the retention interval.

models.name: SecretBundle
models.doc: A secret consisting of a value, id and its attributes.

fields.name: value
fields.doc: The secret value.

fields.name: id
fields.doc: The secret id.

fields.name: content_type
fields.doc: The content type of the secret.

fields.name: attributes
fields.doc: The secret management attributes.

fields.name: tags
fields.doc: Application specific metadata in the form of key-value pairs.

fields.name: kid
fields.doc: If this is a secret backing a KV certificate, then this field specifies the corresponding key backing the KV certificate.

fields.name: managed
fields.doc: True if the secret's lifetime is managed by key vault. If this is a secret backing a certificate, then managed will be true.

fields.name: previous_version
fields.doc: The version of the previous certificate, if applicable. Applies only to certificates created after June 1, 2025. Certificates created before this date are not retroactively updated.

fields.name: error
fields.doc: The key vault server error.

fields.name: code
fields.doc: The error code.

fields.name: message
fields.doc: The error message.

fields.name: inner_error
fields.doc: The key vault server error.

models.name: DeletedSecretBundle
models.doc: A Deleted Secret consisting of its previous id, attributes and its tags, as well as information on when it will be purged.

fields.name: value
fields.doc: The secret value.

fields.name: id
fields.doc: The secret id.

fields.name: content_type
fields.doc: The content type of the secret.

fields.name: attributes
fields.doc: The secret management attributes.

fields.name: tags
fields.doc: Application specific metadata in the form of key-value pairs.

fields.name: kid
fields.doc: If this is a secret backing a KV certificate, then this field specifies the corresponding key backing the KV certificate.

fields.name: managed
fields.doc: True if the secret's lifetime is managed by key vault. If this is a secret backing a certificate, then managed will be true.

fields.name: previous_version
fields.doc: The version of the previous certificate, if applicable. Applies only to certificates created after June 1, 2025. Certificates created before this date are not retroactively updated.

fields.name: recovery_id
fields.doc: The url of the recovery object, used to identify and recover the deleted secret.

fields.name: scheduled_purge_date
fields.doc: The time when the secret is scheduled to be purged, in UTC

fields.name: deleted_date
fields.doc: The time when the secret was deleted, in UTC

fields.name: content_type
fields.doc: Type of the secret value such as a password.

fields.name: secret_attributes
fields.doc: The secret management attributes.

fields.name: tags
fields.doc: Application specific metadata in the form of key-value pairs.

fields.name: value
fields.doc: A response message containing a list of secrets in the key vault along with a link to the next page of secrets.

fields.name: next_link
fields.doc: The URL to get the next set of secrets.

models.name: SecretItem
models.doc: The secret item containing secret metadata.

fields.name: id
fields.doc: Secret identifier.

fields.name: attributes
fields.doc: The secret management attributes.

fields.name: tags
fields.doc: Application specific metadata in the form of key-value pairs.

fields.name: content_type
fields.doc: Type of the secret value such as a password.

fields.name: managed
fields.doc: True if the secret's lifetime is managed by key vault. If this is a key backing a certificate, then managed will be true.

fields.name: value
fields.doc: A response message containing a list of deleted secrets in the key vault along with a link to the next page of deleted secrets.

fields.name: next_link
fields.doc: The URL to get the next set of deleted secrets.

models.name: DeletedSecretItem
models.doc: The deleted secret item containing metadata about the deleted secret.

fields.name: id
fields.doc: Secret identifier.

fields.name: attributes
fields.doc: The secret management attributes.

fields.name: tags
fields.doc: Application specific metadata in the form of key-value pairs.

fields.name: content_type
fields.doc: Type of the secret value such as a password.

fields.name: managed
fields.doc: True if the secret's lifetime is managed by key vault. If this is a key backing a certificate, then managed will be true.

fields.name: recovery_id
fields.doc: The url of the recovery object, used to identify and recover the deleted secret.

fields.name: scheduled_purge_date
fields.doc: The time when the secret is scheduled to be purged, in UTC

fields.name: deleted_date
fields.doc: The time when the secret was deleted, in UTC

models.name: BackupSecretResult
models.doc: The backup secret result, containing the backup blob.

fields.name: value
fields.doc: The backup blob containing the backed up secret.

fields.name: secret_bundle_backup
fields.doc: The backup blob associated with a secret bundle.

enums.name: DeletionRecoveryLevel
enums.doc: Reflects the deletion recovery level currently in effect for secrets in the current vault. If it contains 'Purgeable', the secret can be permanently deleted by a privileged user; otherwise, only the system can purge the secret, at the end of the retention interval.

values.name: Purgeable
values.doc: Denotes a vault state in which deletion is an irreversible operation, without the possibility for recovery. This level corresponds to no protection being available against a Delete operation; the data is irretrievably lost upon accepting a Delete operation at the entity level or higher (vault, resource group, subscription etc.)

values.name: RecoverablePurgeable
values.doc: Denotes a vault state in which deletion is recoverable, and which also permits immediate and permanent deletion (i.e. purge). This level guarantees the recoverability of the deleted entity during the retention interval (90 days), unless a Purge operation is requested, or the subscription is cancelled. System wil permanently delete it after 90 days, if not recovered

values.name: Recoverable
values.doc: Denotes a vault state in which deletion is recoverable without the possibility for immediate and permanent deletion (i.e. purge). This level guarantees the recoverability of the deleted entity during the retention interval (90 days) and while the subscription is still available. System wil permanently delete it after 90 days, if not recovered

values.name: RecoverableProtectedSubscription
values.doc: Denotes a vault and subscription state in which deletion is recoverable within retention interval (90 days), immediate and permanent deletion (i.e. purge) is not permitted, and in which the subscription itself  cannot be permanently canceled. System wil permanently delete it after 90 days, if not recovered

values.name: CustomizedRecoverablePurgeable
values.doc: Denotes a vault state in which deletion is recoverable, and which also permits immediate and permanent deletion (i.e. purge when 7 <= SoftDeleteRetentionInDays < 90). This level guarantees the recoverability of the deleted entity during the retention interval, unless a Purge operation is requested, or the subscription is cancelled.

values.name: CustomizedRecoverable
values.doc: Denotes a vault state in which deletion is recoverable without the possibility for immediate and permanent deletion (i.e. purge when 7 <= SoftDeleteRetentionInDays < 90).This level guarantees the recoverability of the deleted entity during the retention interval and while the subscription is still available.

values.name: CustomizedRecoverableProtectedSubscription
values.doc: Denotes a vault and subscription state in which deletion is recoverable, immediate and permanent deletion (i.e. purge) is not permitted, and in which the subscription itself cannot be permanently canceled when 7 <= SoftDeleteRetentionInDays < 90. This level guarantees the recoverability of the deleted entity during the retention interval, and also reflects the fact that the subscription itself cannot be cancelled.
